vibe: Sunday night deep cuts — warm deep house, shimmering city pop, explosive UK jazz, and Tokyo math rock for late-night vibes in Japan

tracks.name: Jus' Wanna Feel
tracks.reason: Warm, jazzy deep house from one of the genre's best — sits right in that Moodymann/Osunlade pocket you've been living in lately.

tracks.name: Midnight Pretenders
tracks.reason: An absolute city pop gem from 1983 — dreamy synths and Tomoko's silky vocals. If you dig Soichi Terada's vibe, this is the spiritual ancestor.

tracks.name: Falaise
tracks.reason: Sam Shepherd at his most textural and hypnotic — bridges Daft Punk's moodier side with something way more experimental. Proper headphone track.

tracks.name: La cumbia me está llamando (Kaidi Tatham Remix)
tracks.reason: UK jazz meets cumbia with a broken-beat remix from Kaidi Tatham — Nubya's sax over Latin rhythms is an unexpected combo that just works.

tracks.name: Dopamine
tracks.reason: London duo doing disco-funk that feels like Dabeull's cooler cousin. Pure serotonin with a proper groove — perfect transition track.

tracks.name: Tokai
tracks.reason: Produced by Ryuichi Sakamoto in '77 — this is the city pop deep cut that influenced everything. You're in Tokyo, this is basically required listening.

tracks.name: 孤独の発明
tracks.album: the book about my idle plot on a vague anxiety
tracks.reason: Japanese math rock royalty — intricate guitar work that's somehow both technically insane and emotionally warm. Late night headphone essential.

tracks.name: You Are In My System - Club Mix
tracks.reason: Deep house royalty doing what he does best — soulful, driving, and timeless. This is the Kerri Chandler track that should be in every collection.

tracks.name: Qué Pasaría...
tracks.reason: Rauw's smoother, more experimental side from Cosa Nuestra — less trap reggaeton, more silky R&B with Latin flavor. Great palette cleanser.

tracks.name: Chasing the Drum
tracks.reason: Yussef's drumming is otherworldly — this track builds from meditative to explosive. If Surprise Chef's grooves hit for you, this takes it further.
